name: Creamed Corn, Scallion, and Roasted Red Pepper Roulade
recipeIngredient: 4 large eggs, yolks separated, whites at room temperature
1 red bell pepper, roasted and chopped (about 1/2 cup)
3 red bell peppers, roasted and chopped (about 1 1/2 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan melt the butter over low heat. Stir in the flour and cook the roux, stirring, for 3 minutes.
2. Remove the pan from the heat. Add the heated milk in a stream, whisking. Simmer the mixture, whisking occasionally, for 5 minutes. Add the nutmeg, salt, and pepper to taste, and transfer the mixture to a large bowl.
3. Whisk in the egg yolks one at a time, whisking well after each addition. Whisk in the Parmesan.
4. In a bowl with an electric mixer beat the egg whites with a pinch of salt until they are frothy. Add the cream of tartar and beat the whites until they just hold stiff peaks. Stir one third of the whites into the mixture and fold in the remaining whites gently but thoroughly.
5. Spread the sponge batter evenly in a buttered 15 1/2 x 10 1/2-inch jelly-roll pan lined with buttered and floured waxed paper. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for 25 minutes, or until golden and firm to the touch. Cover the egg sponge with a sheet of buttered waxed paper, buttered side down, and a dish towel. Invert a baking sheet over the towel and invert the egg sponge onto the baking sheet. Remove the jelly-roll pan and waxed paper carefully and trim 1/4-inch from the short sides of the egg sponge.
6. Fill the warm egg sponge with the filling while it is still warm and flexible.
7. For the filling: In a skillet combine the corn and 1/2 cup cream, simmer occasionally for 4 minutes. Add salt to taste and cayenne, stir in the scallions and remaining cream, simmer stirring occasionally for 5 minutes, or until thickened and cream is absorbed. Stir in roasted pepper, Parmesan, and season with salt and black pepper.
8. Spread the filling over the warm egg sponge in an even layer, leaving a 1-inch border all around. Roll the roulade jelly-roll fashion, beginning with a long side, and trim the ends on the diagonal.
9. Transfer the roulade, seam side down, to an ovenproof platter, spreading it gently with butter and sprinkling with Parmesan. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for 10 minutes, or until heated through and Parmesan is melted.
10. Puree roasted peppers in a food processor or blender, add salt to taste and cayenne, and transfer the puree to a small bowl.
11. Slice the roulade on the diagonal, serve with the puree, and garnish with parsley sprigs.
Yield: 1 egg sponge (serves 4 as an entree or 8 as a first course)
